description: The verb of completing dialogue, naming the response that answers what inquiry opened. More than providing information, 'lonae' acknowledges that questions create relational obligations—when someone asks genuinely, they deserve genuine response. This word names the moment when communication circles back, when the one asked offers what they can to the one who sought. Whether the response is 'I know' or 'I don't know,' 'lonae' names the honoring of the inquiry through engaged reply.
sound_symbolism: The word opens with 'lo', where the flowing liquid 'l' suggests response arising naturally from question, as stream follows spring. The gathering quality of 'lo'—like the plural marker—collects what the responder knows. This grounds through 'na', double nasal anchoring response in truth and stability—what we respond should be honest, rooted in authentic understanding. The final 'e' opens into clear transmission, the balanced vowel releasing response into shared understanding. The arc traces authentic response: gathering knowledge, grounding in truth, clear release.
grammatical_notes: Functions as both verb and noun. As verb: 'mia lonae tia thilou' (I respond to your inquiry). As noun: 'wela lonae' (good response). Distinguished from 'shemui' (utter) by implying completion of prior communication. Often appears in parallel with 'thilou' (inquire) when discussing dialogue.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Thoughtful response rather than reaction supports peaceful dialogue. 'Lonae' names the completing half of conversational exchange, encouraging speakers to answer with care rather than defensiveness.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Mindful response requires presence—hearing fully before answering. 'Lonae' supports this practice through its deliberate structure, creating space between question and reply.

tags.dialogue: completing the arc of verbal exchange
